title: 8 Herbal Creams For Insomnia
intro_desc: If you're having trouble sleeping, you might want to try using herbal creams that contain calming herbs. These creams can help your body relax and prepare for sleep. Herbs like Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, have been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. It's believed that the active compounds in valerian root help slow down your heart rate and make you feel drowsy.  Another herb that's often used in insomnia treatments is Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower. This herb has been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep. Some studies have even found that passionflower is as effective as prescription sleep aids in promoting relaxation.  Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender, is another calming herb that's often used in herbal creams. The scent of lavender is known to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep. When applied topically, lavender oil can help calm your mind and body, preparing you for a restful night's sleep.  Using herbal creams for insomnia can have several benefits. For one, they're a natural and non-habit-forming way to promote sleep. Unlike prescription sleep aids, which can be addictive, herbal creams are a safer and healthier option. Additionally, herbal creams can be used in combination with other relaxation techniques, such as meditation or deep breathing, to promote a restful night's sleep. By using herbal creams, you can improve the quality of your sleep and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid and valepotriates, which are the main active constituents that help with insomnia. These compounds have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. Valerenic acid also has a calming effect on the brain, slowing down the activity of neurons and promoting a restful sleep. The valepotriates in Valeriana officinalis creams have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep. By reducing stress and anxiety and promoting relaxation, Valeriana officinalis creams can help individuals with insomnia to get a good night's sleep.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis root, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the dried Valeriana officinalis root in 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut oil) for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture every day.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids. Add the beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the infused oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter melt. Stir well and remove from heat.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Valeriana officinalis cream for insomnia is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides as its bioactive constituents. The flavonoids, particularly flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm the mind and body before sleep. The alkaloids, such as harmine and harmaline, have a central nervous system depressant effect, which can help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The glycosides, including passifloric acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that may contribute to improved sleep quality by reducing inflammation and promoting a restful night's sleep. By combining these properties, Passiflora incarnata creams may help individuals with insomnia by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add the dried Passiflora incarnata flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents that have been shown to promote relaxation. These compounds work by reducing anxiety and stress, which are common causes of insomnia. The sedative properties of linalool and linalyl acetate help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. The antispasmodic properties of these compounds also help to relax tense muscles, further contributing to a restful night's sleep. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help individuals with insomnia to get the restful sleep they need.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Heat the mixture for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has turned a light purple color and the flowers have lost their fragrance.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.

Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has cooled slightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, which are responsible for its calming properties. These compounds help to reduce stress and anxiety, common causes of insomnia. Rosmarinic acid also has a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension. Apigenin binds to GABA receptors in the brain, which can lead to a sense of calmness and tranquility. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, Melissa officinalis creams can help improve the quality of sleep.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Melissa officinalis leaves and 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the coconut oil in the saucepan over low heat for 2-3 hours.
Add the dried Melissa officinalis leaves to the coconut oil and let it steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves. Let the oil cool down.

Mix 2 tablespoons of the cooled oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to create a creamy texture. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams contains a compound called avenacoside, which is also known as 5-hydroxyavanine. This compound is derived from the oat plant and has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress. Avenacoside has sedative properties that help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, avena sativa creams also contain other bioactive constituents such as avenanthramides, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe the skin and promote relaxation. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, avena sativa creams can help to alleviate insomnia symptoms and improve sleep quality.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Combine the dried Avena sativa flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan, heat over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture and discard the Avena sativa flowers, then add the beeswax and heat until melted.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes, then stir until the mixture thickens.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and solidify, then store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation. The sedative effect of ginger is due to its ability to regulate the body's circadian rhythms by influencing the production of certain neurotransmitters. By promoting a restful state, ginger creams can improve the quality of sleep and help alleviate insomnia symptoms. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams may lead to improved sleep patterns and reduced sleep disturbances.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger root powder (Zingiber officinale), and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the ginger root powder and shea butter to the melted mixture. Stir well until the powder is fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches around 120°F to 130°F.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, the cream is ready to use for insomnia relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus creams contains boiactive constituents like humulone and lupulone, which have sedative properties that help with insomnia. These compounds work by slowing down brain activity, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. The sedative properties of humulone and lupulone also help to reduce anxiety and stress, common causes of insomnia. Additionally, the creams contain other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to regulate the body's sleep-wake cycle. By reducing stress and regulating the body's sleep-wake cycle, Humulus lupulus creams can provide relief from insomnia and promote a good night's sleep.
remedies.image_alt: Humulus lupulus herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Humulus lupulus flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Humulus lupulus flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the Humulus lupulus cream in the fridge and use 1-2 tablespoons as needed before bedtime.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds work together to improve blood circulation and oxygenation to the brain, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can lead to insomnia. The flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba also have antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to increase the production of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that regulates sleep, can help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to improve the quality of sleep and reduce the frequency of insomnia episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for insomnia
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in Ginkgo biloba extract and lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, use as a night cream for insomnia relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with sleep. Certain teas, like chamomile and lavender, have a calming effect that can slow down your mind and body. This can make it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. The soothing flavor and aroma of herbal tea can also create a relaxing atmosphere, making it a good way to unwind before bed.
faq_safe: Herbal teas for insomnia can be safe to consume daily, but it's essential to use them responsibly. Some teas, like chamomile, have a calming effect that can help with sleep. However, drinking them too frequently can lead to dependency, especially if you're relying on them to fall asleep.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for insomnia can start to help within a few days to a week. Some people notice a difference in their sleep after just a night or two. It really depends on the individual and the type of herbal tea they're using. It's also possible that it may take a bit longer to see results.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for insomnia can be helpful in the evening, about an hour before bedtime. This allows the calming effects of the tea to take hold, making it easier to fall asleep. The body can then relax and prepare for a restful night's sleep, reducing the likelihood of insomnia.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas, like valerian root and chamomile, can affect how prescription sleep medications work in your body. This might make them stronger or last longer, which can be a problem if you're taking them to help you sleep. It's also possible for herbal teas to interact with other medications, like blood thinners or diabetes pills.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some teas, like those with valerian root, chamomile, and lavender, are known to calm the body and mind. However, their effects on the baby are not fully understood. It's best to talk to your body and see how you react to small amounts of these teas before consuming more.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a natural way to help with insomnia. However, when breastfeeding, it's best to check the ingredients first. Some herbal teas, like chamomile and lavender, are generally considered safe in moderation. But others, like valerian root and passionflower, can pass into breast milk and affect the baby.
title_2: 8 Herbal Creams to Help Older Adults Overcome Insomnia
